metadata.description:
Batch-fix 15 remaining test failures on fix/fda-readiness-improvement-xml using proper GATHER→CATEGORIZE→PLAN→FIX→VERIFY methodology (Rule #23). Also: improve self-learning-iterative-coder skill to prevent whac-a-mole.
METALEARNING REFERENCE: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-18-whac-a-mole-serial-failure-fixing.md CLAUDE.md Rule #23: NEVER Fix Failures Serially
metadata.prior-work: Phase 1-4 of fda-insights-second-pass-executable.xml COMPLETE. OpenLineage wired into 5 flows, CycloneDX SBOM added, KG updated, 5 GitHub issues created. 4614 tests pass, 15 remain.
metadata.cold-start-instructions: 1. git checkout fix/fda-readiness-improvement-xml 2. git pull 3. Read this XML plan 4. Execute Phase 1 (batch fix), then Phase 2 (skill improvement) 5. Run make test-staging — must be ZERO failures 6. git push 7. Wait for user command before creating PR

root-cause.name: slash-prefix-metric-keys
root-cause.description: MLflow slash-prefix migration (#790) changed metric/param key format from underscore (eval_fold0_dsc) to slash (eval/fold0/dsc). Tests still assert old underscore keys. This is the SAME root cause that affected test_system_info.py (19 tests) and test_observability.py (10+ tests) — already fixed in earlier commits on this branch.

file.name: tests/v2/unit/test_trainer_metrics.py
file.failure: train_dice → train/dice in logged metrics set

root-cause.fix-strategy: Batch sed-like replacement of underscore keys to slash keys in all 3 test files. Use ast.parse to verify syntax after replacement. Do NOT replace function names or variable names — only string literals used as dict keys or assertion targets.

root-cause.name: checkpoint-naming-changed
root-cause.description: Trainer refactor changed checkpoint naming from best_val_loss.pth to a different convention. 6 tests in test_trainer_multi_metric.py expect the old best_*.pth pattern. The trainer now uses a different naming scheme for checkpoints (possibly metric-based subdirectories or different filename patterns).

file.name: tests/v2/unit/test_trainer_multi_metric.py
file.failure: test_early_stop_triggered: Expected early stop before epoch 20

root-cause.fix-strategy: 1. Read the actual Trainer.fit() code to understand current checkpoint naming convention 2. Update ALL 6 tests to match the actual convention 3. For early_stop_triggered: check if early stopping logic changed 4. Single commit for all 6 fixes

root-cause.name: skypilot-yaml-restructured
root-cause.description: SkyPilot YAML files were restructured (DVC remote strategy change, timing instrumentation). Tests expect DVC_REMOTE env var and dvc_pull timing markers that may have been moved or renamed.

file.name: tests/v2/unit/test_skypilot_configs.py
file.failure: smoke_test_gpu.yaml setup must include dvc pull -r remote_storage

root-cause.fix-strategy: 1. Read the actual SkyPilot YAML files to see current structure 2. Update tests to match actual YAML content 3. If DVC_REMOTE was intentionally removed, update test expectations 4. Single commit for all 4 fixes

phase.name: batch-fix-remaining-failures
phase.methodology: Rule #23 — GATHER → CATEGORIZE → PLAN → FIX → VERIFY

step.name: GATHER
step.command: MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 PREFECT_DISABLED=1 uv run pytest tests/ \ -m "not model_loading and not slow and not integration and not gpu and not gpu_heavy" \ --ignore=tests/gpu_instance --ignore=tests/v2/cloud \ -q --tb=line --maxfail=200 -p no:warnings 2>&1 | grep "FAILED"
step.acceptance: Exactly 15 failures matching the inventory above.

step.name: FIX-RC1: Slash-prefix metric keys (5 tests)
step.approach: For each file: 1. Read the file 2. Identify ALL string literals that are dict keys with underscore format (eval_fold0_dsc, prof_first_epoch_seconds, train_dice, etc.) 3. Map each to the slash-prefix equivalent using the MetricKeys class in src/minivess/observability/metric_keys.py as the source of truth 4. Replace ALL at once (not one-by-one) 5. Verify syntax with ast.parse() 6. Run the specific test file to confirm GREEN
step.commit-message: fix: batch slash-prefix migration for tracker_evaluation, timing, trainer_metrics (#790)

step.name: FIX-RC2: Checkpoint naming (6 tests)
step.approach: 1. Read src/minivess/pipeline/trainer.py to find the ACTUAL checkpoint saving code — what filename pattern does it use? 2. Read all 6 failing test methods to understand what they expect 3. Update ALL 6 tests to match the actual trainer behavior 4. For early_stop_triggered: check if patience/threshold changed 5. Run test_trainer_multi_metric.py to confirm all 6 GREEN
step.commit-message: fix: update checkpoint naming assertions to match refactored trainer

step.name: FIX-RC3: SkyPilot YAML content (4 tests)
step.approach: 1. Read the actual SkyPilot YAML files 2. Check if DVC_REMOTE env var exists or was renamed/removed 3. Check if dvc_pull timing markers exist 4. Update tests to match reality OR add missing YAML content 5. Run all 3 test files to confirm GREEN
step.commit-message: fix: align SkyPilot YAML tests with current deployment configs

step.name: PUSH
step.note: Do NOT create PR — wait for user command.

phase.name: improve-tdd-skill
phase.description: Add a MANDATORY "Failure Triage Protocol" to the self-learning-iterative-coder skill so that future test-fix cycles NEVER use the whac-a-mole pattern. This compounds the metalearning from this session into the skill itself.

step.name: Add failure-triage protocol
step.content-outline:
# Failure Triage Protocol (Rule #23 Enforcement)
## WHEN: After any test suite run with failures
## MANDATORY STEPS:
### Step 1: GATHER (one pass, no stopping) Run: pytest --maxfail=200 WITHOUT -x Capture ALL failures, not just the first one.
### Step 2: CATEGORIZE (group by root cause) Command: grep "FAILED" | sed 's/::.*//g' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn Then: Read failure messages to identify root causes. Output: Table of root causes with file counts.
### Step 3: PLAN (one fix per root cause, not per test) For each root cause: - Identify the SOURCE OF TRUTH (e.g., MetricKeys class for key names) - Plan a batch replacement/fix - Estimate affected test count
### Step 4: FIX (batch, one commit per root cause) - Fix ALL instances of each root cause in one pass - Verify syntax with ast.parse() after text replacements - Run only the affected test files to confirm GREEN
### Step 5: VERIFY (full suite, no -x) Run the full suite again to confirm no regressions.
## ANTI-PATTERNS (BANNED): - Running with -x and fixing one test at a time - Committing after each individual test fix - Not reading the source of truth before fixing tests - Replacing strings without checking for function name corruption - Using replace_all without verifying no Python identifiers are affected
## ESCALATION: If the same root cause appears in 10+ files, create an agent to do the batch replacement rather than doing it manually.

step.name: Update SKILL.md to reference failure-triage
step.change: Add failure-triage.md to the Protocol Reference table. Add "FAILURE TRIAGE MANDATORY" to Critical Rules section. Reference CLAUDE.md Rule #23 and the metalearning doc.

step.name: Update verify-phase protocol
step.change: Add: "If verification reveals failures, STOP. Do not proceed to fix-phase. Instead, invoke the Failure Triage Protocol (failure-triage.md) to gather ALL failures before fixing ANY of them."

step.name: Update fix-phase protocol
step.change: Add: "Before fixing any test failure, check if this is the ONLY failure. If there are multiple failures, you MUST run the Failure Triage Protocol first. Fixing one failure at a time is the #1 time-wasting anti-pattern in this project (see metalearning 2026-03-18)."

step.name: Commit skill improvements
step.commit-message:
chore(skill): add failure-triage protocol to self-learning-iterative-coder
Enforces CLAUDE.md Rule #23: GATHER→CATEGORIZE→PLAN→FIX→VERIFY. Prevents whac-a-mole serial failure fixing that wastes 25+ minutes on what should be a 10-minute batch operation.
Metalearning: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-18-whac-a-mole-serial-failure-fixing.md

success-criteria.criterion: All 15 failures fixed in exactly 3 commits (one per root cause)
failure-triage.md protocol exists in skill directory
verify-phase.md and fix-phase.md updated with triage gate
NO PR created (wait for user command)
